ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To Constantius, Bishop.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Constantius, Bishop of Mediolanum (Milan).

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: On receiving the letters of your Fraternity I returned great thanks to Almighty God, that I was counted worthy to be refreshed by the celebration of your ordination.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Truly that all, by the gift of God, with one accord concurred in your election, is a fact which thy Fraternity ought with the utmost consideration to estimate, since, after God, you are greatly indebted to those who with so submissive a disposition desired you to be preferred before themselves.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: It becomes you, therefore, with priestly benignity to respond to their behaviour, and with kind sympathy to attend to their needs.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: If perchance there are any faults in any of them, rebuke these with well-considered reproofs, so that your very priestly indignation be mingled with a savour of sweetness, and that so you may be loved by your subjects even when you are greatly feared.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Such conduct will also induce great reverence for your person in their judgment; since, as hasty and habitual rage is despised, so discriminate indignation against faults for the most part becomes the formidable in proportion as it has been slow.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Further, John our subdeacon, who has returned, has reported many good things of you as to which we beseech Almighty God Himself to fulfil what He has begun; to the end that He may shew thee to have advanced in good inwardly and outwardly both now among men and hereafter among the angels.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Moreover, we have sent thee, according to custom, a pallium to be used in the sacred solemnities of mass.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But I beg you, when you receive it, to vindicate its dignity and its meaning by humility.
